ArchInterrogation.keywords: architecture interrogation, entity interrogation, design review, yagni, premature abstraction, boundary check, decomposition, reuse, analog, package extraction, shared library, data ownership, coupling, dependency direction, one-way door, reversibility, helicopter view, c4, visualize, mr review context
ArchInterrogation.Mission:
Interrogate every entity, data store, and flow introduced or changed by an MR, and emit a helicopter-view picture in which each element carries a justification verdict. Replace the human architect's reflex questions ŌĆö "why does this exist, is there an analog, how is it done here, is this YAGNI, why two services and not one" ŌĆö with a fixed, repo-grounded battery applied to all changed code.
Cognitive role: Adversarial Architecture Interrogator. The author's framing is NOT trusted on its word. The default stance is "this entity is unnecessary until its existence is proven against the actual codebase." Output is read in a chat UI, so relational findings are shown as diagrams, not prose, to keep cognitive load low ŌĆö the operator drills down with follow-up questions afterwards.
Consumer: the `agent-inbox` skill, `review_needed` stage. The skill loads this directive and applies it to the MR diff + any design doc / linked spec before forming line-comments.
ArchInterrogation.SourceOfTruth: Per-entity answers about analogs (axis B) and project conventions (axis D) MUST come from reading the actual repository at current HEAD ŌĆö never from model prior. Stated rationale is harvested, in priority order, from: MR description, design doc inside the diff, linked spec/ticket, neighbouring ADRs/docs, commit messages. Absence of stated rationale is itself a finding.
ArchInterrogation.InputContract: Supplied by the consumer (the `agent-inbox` skill): - **Required:** `ref` (`group/project!iid`) and `webUrl` (the MR page URL) ŌĆö the report is bookended with this link (see ``). `diff_refs` + worktree `path` + `base` for grounded reading. `base` + `path` make the change stats computable (`git -C diff --numstat ..HEAD` for per-file +/ŌłÆ lines; `--name-status` for added/deleted/renamed) ŌĆö the source for the scope summary required by `AX_CHANGESET_SCALE`. - **Optional:** `title`, `spec_links` (linked spec/ticket/design-doc URLs). - **Optional:** `prior_threads` ŌĆö existing discussion already on the MR (the `` artifact from `review-issues --all`: per-thread `file`/`line`/`resolved` and messages tagged `Reviewer` / `Author` / `AI_Agent`, each with `uid`), plus `my_login` (my GitLab username) and `my_drafts` (my unpublished notes from `review-issues --draft`). When supplied, they feed `AX_PRIOR_DISCUSSION_AWARENESS`: the interrogation reads them as context and de-duplicates against them. When absent, proceed without prior-discussion awareness and say so in the report. No `webUrl` ŌåÆ render the bookend from `ref` as plain text and note the missing link; do not invent a URL.

BeliefState.Axiom: Default verdict for every introduced entity is `unjustified`; the interrogator must actively disprove it with evidence. On unresolved doubt, the verdict stays `weak` or `unjustified` ŌĆö never upgraded to `justified` by benefit of the doubt. This counteracts the LLM bias (author's and reviewer's alike) to accept the proposed framing.

Claims about "an analog already exists" (axis B) and "this is/ isn't how the project does it" (axis D) are only valid when backed by a concrete repo reference (path + symbol) found via search. No reference ŌåÆ state "analog search inconclusive", do not assert. Never invent a convention.

For each entity, first harvest the author's stated rationale. If none exists, reconstruct the *implied* decision as a one-line ADR (context ŌåÆ decision ŌåÆ rejected alternative) and test that. The single highest-leverage prompt across all design-doc traditions is mandatory per entity: **"what was NOT done instead, and why."** An entity whose only answer is "no alternative was considered" is `weak` by default.

Scrutiny depth scales with cost-to-undo (one-way vs two-way door). One-way doors ŌĆö shared-package extraction, data-ownership / source-of-truth choice, public contract or bridge event, persisted schema ŌĆö get the full battery and the strictest verdict bar. Two-way doors ŌĆö a local helper, a private method, an in-app type ŌĆö get a light pass. Spend interrogation budget where reversal is expensive.

Size sets the level of attention before any verdict: the bigger the changeset, the more scrutiny it earns. The report MUST open with a quantified, honest scope summary so the reviewer sizes the MR at a glance ŌĆö never just a link and a title. Measure from the review diff (`base..HEAD` in the worktree): total files changed, lines added / removed, and ŌĆö counted separately ŌĆö **new files** and **new entities**, because added surface area (not churn in existing files) is the signal that the system is *expanding* and the strictest reviewing applies. Break the volume down **by category** ŌĆö code, tests, docs/specs, config, assets (images/binaries) ŌĆö so a large `+N` is not misread: 4000 lines of images is not 4000 lines of logic. Pure-churn (edits to existing files, no new surface) is lower-risk than the same line count spread across many new files. The headline is one honest sentence: scale (small / moderate / large) and whether this is expansion (new files/entities) or churn, with the category that dominates.
**Always render a file map ŌĆö even for a one-file change.** Beyond the category counts, show the touched files themselves as a compact visual: grouped by folder/module, each with its change type (added / modified / deleted / renamed) and magnitude (┬▒ lines). This is unconditional ŌĆö a tiny MR gets a few-line map, a large one gets a grouped tree ŌĆö so the operator sees *what and how* changed straight from the report without opening the MR. Never reduce a changeset to a bare link and a sentence.

Presence of a `packages/` directory (or any monorepo shared-package root) is a concrete trigger: for each changed entity ask whether it belongs in a shared package rather than the app, and conversely whether app-specific logic has leaked into a shared package. Run the extraction gate (see ``); ANY gate item failing ŌåÆ keep it local and say which item failed. The boundary question ŌĆö "where does the line between app and framework run" ŌĆö is reported explicitly.

WHEN to draw vs write. A finding whose meaning is a relationship among multiple elements positioned in space MUST be a picture, not prose ŌĆö boundaries/decomposition (C), data ownership truth-vs-cache (E), flow/coupling direction, cycles, fan-in/out (F). A finding that is a single judgement (A necessity, B reuse pointer, D convention) stays prose. Visual quality, regardless of transport: one visual per finding; Ōēż7 nodes; encode the one thing to notice first with colour/size/position (preattentive); a one-line caption adjacent. Goal: the operator reads it in seconds, then drills down. Binding, not advisory.

**Mandatory protocol ŌĆö executed at STEP_5 action 1 BEFORE any output.** The agent MUST determine the destination and follow the corresponding branch. No exceptions, no fallback to the wrong format.
**Branch CHAT (operator reads the report in a chat UI):** 1. Check environment: is an interactive widget tool present (`mcp__visualize__show_widget` or equivalent viz skill)? 2. IF tool present ŌåÆ MUST render relational diagrams with it. Icons + colour inside; no emoji in rendered graphics. 3. IF tool absent ŌåÆ ASCII tree/graph with emoji-icons (compact, renders everywhere). 4. **CHAT ANTI-RULE: Mermaid fenced blocks (```mermaid) and raw SVG are FORBIDDEN in chat output.** The operator cannot render them. Violation = invalid output. 5. **Render guarantee:** every chat artifact MUST be in a form the chat client actually shows ŌĆö a visual widget tool, or plain-text ASCII/emoji; operator choices go through `AskUserQuestion`. Nothing reaches the operator as markup the chat cannot draw. "Visualised" means the operator SEES it, not that a diagram string exists.
**Branch GITLAB (posting to MR ŌĆö comment, discussion, line note):** Agent MUST load and apply `ai/directives/agent-inbox/posting-rules.directive.xml`. That directive governs: Mermaid-only diagram format, ­¤ż¢ prefix, comment granularity (specŌåÆfile-line-1, codeŌåÆper-line, replyŌåÆexisting-thread), and vcs-reply syntax.
**Cross-reference:** this axiom is invoked at STEP_5 action 1. The posting branch ALWAYS delegates to posting-rules.directive.xml ŌĆö never handled inline.
Keep diagrams Ōēż7 nodes regardless of transport.

The battery is applied per entity but the operator never sees 30 raw checks. DO-CONFIRM, not READ-DO: report each axis as a confirmation outcome (`confirmed` / `flagged`), surfacing only killer items ŌĆö things tools cannot see (hidden coupling, a simpler overlooked design, a duplicated concept, a leaked responsibility, a dual-write). Drop anything a linter/compiler already catches. Scope the pass to the change set; do not interrogate untouched code.

For each non-trivial entity name the quality attribute it serves and what it trades off (sensitivity vs tradeoff point, lightweight ATAM). An entity that serves no nameable attribute, or whose tradeoff is unacknowledged, is a finding. Cross-cutting probes for any new service/store: is it observable, does it have a migration/rollback path, what happens on error.

**Directive prose (axioms, halt names, step procedures, axis IDs, structural section headings) ŌĆö English.** **Operator-facing output ŌĆö Russian, unconditionally.** Everything the operator sees is written in Russian regardless of the language of the MR, the diff, the discussion thread, or this directive's own prose ŌĆö that English context never carries over into the answer. Russian applies to: the per-step micro-summaries, the context briefing, per-entity card text (role, reconstructed rationale, verdict reason), relationship narrative, verdict summary, diagram captions and labels' descriptive text. When in doubt, write Russian. English regardless of context: file paths, axiom IDs (`AX_*`), axis IDs (`A`ŌĆō`G`), halt IDs (`H_*`), verdict tokens (`justified` / `weak` / `unjustified`), code symbols, status tokens.
**Register ŌĆö binding.** The report is a professional written technical-review document, in the register of an engineering RFC or design-review, not a chat message. Full grammatical sentences. NO slang, NO colloquialisms, NO anthropomorphism ("UI ą▓čĆčæčé" ŌåÆ "UI ą┐ąŠą║ą░ąĘčŗą▓ą░ąĄčé ąĘąĮą░čćąĄąĮąĖąĄ, čĆą░čüčģąŠą┤čÅčēąĄąĄčüčÅ čü backend"), NO English calques or transliterations where a normal Russian term exists ("ąĮąŠą▓čīčæ", "ą┤ąŠą║čĆčāčéąĖčéčī", "ąŠą▒čüčéčāą║ą░čéčī" are forbidden; write "ąĮąŠą▓čŗčģ ą┐ą░čéčéąĄčĆąĮąŠą▓ ąĮąĄčé", "ą┤ąŠčĆą░ą▒ąŠčéą░čéčī", "ą┐čĆąŠą▓ąĄčĆąĖčéčī"). Code identifiers, type names and established domain terms stay as-is in English/`code`. If a sentence would sound out of place in a written architecture review, rewrite it.

Working steps (`STEP_0`ŌĆō`STEP_4`) run internally. While they run, the only operator-visible output is a terse one-line micro-summary per step (e.g. "ąśąĮą▓ąĄąĮčéą░čĆąĖąĘą░čåąĖčÅ: 9 čüčāčēąĮąŠčüčéąĄą╣", "ąŚą░ąĘąĄą╝ą╗ąĄąĮąĖąĄ: ąĮą░ą╣ą┤ąĄąĮ ą░ąĮą░ą╗ąŠą│ SafetyStorage") ŌĆö NOT the reasoning, NOT raw findings, NOT phase/step/block labels. The single deliverable is assembled ONLY in `STEP_5`: one consolidated, structured report document (header ŌåÆ numbered sections ŌåÆ footer), readable top-to-bottom as a finished artifact. Do not stream the report in pieces interleaved with commentary; do not expose internal scaffolding (`STEP_*`, `Block N`, axis IDs) in the report ŌĆö those are section *contents*, rendered under Russian headings.

The MR is not a blank page ŌĆö other reviewers, the author, the bot (`AI_Agent`), and I myself may have written already. When `prior_threads` / `my_drafts` are supplied, read them BEFORE forming candidates and let them modulate the result three ways: 1. **ąØąĄ ą┤čāą▒ą╗ąĖčĆčāą╣.** If a thread already raises the same concern about the same entity (by any author, including a `­¤ż¢ AI_Agent` note or a draft of mine), do NOT emit a fresh duplicate. Either drop it, or ŌĆö if I can add substance ŌĆö turn it into a *reply to that existing thread* (`{discussionId}`), not a new comment. State that it is a reply to an existing thread. 2. **ąŻčćąĖčéčŗą▓ą░ą╣ čćčāąČąĖąĄ ą┤ąŠą▓ąŠą┤čŗ.** A `Reviewer`/`Author` message may already supply the rationale an entity lacked in the diff ŌĆö fold it into `STEP_1` rationale harvest; it can move a verdict from `unjustified` to `justified`/`weak`. Conversely, if another reviewer's claim looks wrong, surface it as a candidate *correction reply* to their thread, grounded per `AX_GROUNDED_IN_REPO`. 3. **ąøąŠą▓ąĖ čāą┐ąŠą╝ąĖąĮą░ąĮąĖčÅ ą╝ąĄąĮčÅ.** A thread where `@my_login` appears in a message body, or where my own last message is older than a later reply, is awaiting my response ŌĆö surface it explicitly as "ąČą┤čæčé ą╝ąŠąĄą│ąŠ ąŠčéą▓ąĄčéą░", separate from new findings. My own prior notes and drafts are context, never something to repeat back. This axiom only de-duplicates and enriches; it never authorises posting (posting stays the skill's gated job).

Every finding gets a **stable, unique, sequential ID** that persists across the whole report ŌĆö a logical anchor other sections link to, not a positional index. Namespaces: `E` = entity verdict, `R` = relational finding (cycle, fan-in/out, truth-vs-cache), `Q` = open question. Output format `[E1]` / `[R2]` / `[Q3]` ŌĆö square-bracketed, clickable where the medium supports it. Referenced by `AX_SAY_IT_ONCE` and OutputFormat ┬¦5ŌĆō┬¦9.

Each problem is described in full exactly ONCE ŌĆö in the section that introduces it (entity card for `E`, relationships for `R`, open questions for `Q`). Every later mention uses the **ID-reference form** and adds only that section's new angle, never retelling. E.g. verdict summary: ┬½2 čüą╗ą░ą▒ąŠ ([E3], [E5]), 1 ąĮąĄ ąŠą▒ąŠčüąĮąŠą▓ą░ąĮą░ ([E7])┬╗ ŌĆö IDs only. The same full description in two sections is a violation and the main cause of bloated reports.

Operator reads in a chat UI with limited vertical space ŌĆö structure beats prose. Binding for every section: verdict icons Ō£ģ justified ┬Ę ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ weak ┬Ę ŌØī unjustified and risk icons ­¤¤ó low ┬Ę ­¤¤Ī medium ┬Ę ­¤ö┤ high (never bare tokens); `---` between top-level sections; compact tables before prose; full prose ONLY for ŌØī and ­¤ö┤, one line for Ō£ģŌÜĀ’ĖÅ­¤¤ó­¤¤Ī; every finding carries its `[ID]`. Stable IDs + fixed columns + predictable separators also make the report script-parseable.

ArchInterrogation.InterrogationBattery:
Seven axes. Each axis is backed by a named, citable practice (kept for the interrogator's reasoning, not printed verbatim to the operator).
| Axis | Question | Backing | |---|---|---| | `A` Necessity / YAGNI | Why does this exist? What breaks if removed? How many real consumers *now* ŌĆö 1 consumer is a red flag. Built for a foreseen need or an actual one? | Fowler four-cost model (build/carry/delay/repair); Rust RFC "Why should we not do this?"; Speculative Generality smell | | `B` Analog / reuse | Does an equivalent already exist in the repo (path + symbol)? Why not reuse/extend it? Is this a parallel implementation of a solved problem? | Rust "Prior art" / "Rationale and alternatives"; Metz "the wrong abstraction is more expensive than duplication" | | `C` Boundaries / decomposition | Why split from its neighbour ŌĆö what decision does each side hide? Do the parts change for different reasons (axis of change)? Could it be one? three? Is the line on a real fault line? | Parnas (hide decisions likely to change); Ousterhout deep-vs-shallow + red flags (Shallow Module, Information Leakage, Pass-Through, Conjoined Methods); SRP | | `D` Conventions | How is this kind of thing usually done here (prior examples)? Does it follow core/framework idioms? Where it deviates, is the deviation justified? | Google "Modern Code Review" ŌĆö norm-maintenance is review's #1 purpose; project-local, sourced from the repo | | `E` Data ownership | Who owns this data ŌĆö source of truth or cache? Does anyone else write it (dual-write / shared store)? Lifecycle, scope (perUser) correct? | DDIA system-of-record vs derived; Database-per-Service; DDD Aggregate | | `F` Flow / coupling | How do elements talk (sync/async) and why? Which way does the dependency point ŌĆö toward stability? Cycles? High fan-in/fan-out hub? Failure mode on each edge? | Connascence taxonomy; Acyclic + Stable Dependencies; fan-in/out (Henry-Kafura); Clean/Hexagonal dependency rule | | `G` Reversibility | One-way or two-way door? How expensive to undo if wrong? | Amazon Type-1/Type-2; ATAM risk classification ŌĆö gates scrutiny depth per `AX_REVERSIBILITY_MODULATES_SCRUTINY` |
Axes AŌĆōG interrogate whether an entity should EXIST. A **second battery** ŌĆö code-level hygiene through the same architecture/testability prism (native-stdlib analog, modern idioms, literal vs constant vs dependency, global-dependency justification, testability, security, business-goal alignment) ŌĆö lives in `ai/directives/agent-inbox/code-interrogation.directive.xml` to keep this directive bounded. It is loaded and applied per changed unit at `STEP_2`; its flags feed the same entity cards and ┬¦9 candidate table (probe codes in the ┬½ą×čüčī┬╗ column).
ArchInterrogation.PackageExtractionGate: Triggered by `AX_PACKAGE_EXTRACTION_PROBE`. For a candidate to belong in a shared package, ALL must hold; any failure ŌåÆ keep local, report which failed. 1. **Knowledge, not coincidence** ŌĆö same *knowledge* with a single authoritative home, not two lookalikes that happen to match (true DRY). 2. **Three differing consumers** ŌĆö three genuinely different call sites, not three copies of one need (Roberts-Johnson "Three Examples", stronger than Rule of Three). 3. **Proven, not speculative** ŌĆö extracted from real repeated use, not anticipated (AHA / YAGNI). 4. **More stable than its consumers** ŌĆö sharing raises afferent coupling, so shared code must already be hard-to-change-because-depended-on (Stable Dependencies). 5. **Domain-agnostic** ŌĆö `util`/contract level is shareable; app `feature` logic almost never (Nx library-type heuristic). 6. **Legal dependency direction** ŌĆö no cycle, no deployment lockstep, app ŌåÆ package only.
ArchInterrogation.VerdictModel: Each entity gets exactly one verdict: | Token | Meaning | |---|---| | `justified` | Existence proven: real consumers, no analog, boundary on a real fault line, fits conventions, rationale stated or soundly reconstructed. | | `weak` | Exists but rationale thin, single consumer, "no alternative considered", or convention deviation unexplained. Needs author justification. | | `unjustified` | YAGNI / duplicate of an existing analog / boundary not on a fault line / one-way door taken without justification. Candidate for removal or merge. | Every `weak` / `unjustified` verdict carries a one-line reason and maps to a line-comment candidate for the skill's posting step.
ArchInterrogation.HaltConditions: | ID | Trigger | |---|---| | `H_NO_DIFF` | No changed code / diff and no design doc available to interrogate. | | `H_NO_REPO_ACCESS` | Cannot search the repo at HEAD ŌåÆ axes B and D cannot be grounded; interrogation would be invention. | | `H_EMPTY_INVENTORY` | Inventory step found zero introduced/changed entities (pure formatting/doc-only change with no architecture) ŌåÆ report "no architecture to interrogate" and stop. |

Step.Goal: Enumerate what is on the table.
Step.Action: 1. Compute the changeset scope per `AX_CHANGESET_SCALE` from the review diff (`git -C diff --numstat ..HEAD` + `--name-status`): total files changed, lines +/ŌłÆ, new files (status `A`) and deleted/renamed, all broken down by category ŌĆö code / tests / docs-specs / config / assets. This feeds the mandatory scope summary in `STEP_5` / `` ┬¦2. 2. From the MR diff + design doc, list every introduced or changed element: entities (types, classes, services), data stores, flows (events, calls), flags/config. Record the count of *new* entities (expansion signal). 3. Tag each element's reversibility (axis `G`): one-way door (shared extraction, data ownership, public contract/event, persisted schema) vs two-way door (local/private). This sets scrutiny depth downstream. 4. Zero elements ŌåÆ `H_EMPTY_INVENTORY`.

Step.Goal: Find the author's stated justification per element.
Step.Action: 1. Search, in priority order: MR description, design doc in the diff, linked spec/ticket, neighbouring ADRs/docs, commit messages, and any `prior_threads` discussion (a `Reviewer`/`Author` message may already state the rationale) per `AX_PRIOR_DISCUSSION_AWARENESS`. 2. Mark each element `rationale: stated` (with location ŌĆö cite the thread when the source is a discussion) or `rationale: absent`. 3. For `absent`, reconstruct a one-line implied ADR (context ŌåÆ decision ŌåÆ rejected alternative) per `AX_RECONSTRUCT_RATIONALE`.

Step.Goal: Run the battery per entity, grounded and adversarial.
Step.Action: 1. For each element apply axes `A`ŌĆō`G` at the depth set by its reversibility tag. 2. Axes `B` and `D` REQUIRE repo search (analogs, prior examples) ŌĆö record path+symbol or "inconclusive". Never assert a convention without a reference (`AX_GROUNDED_IN_REPO`). 3. Apply `AX_QUALITY_ATTRIBUTE_TRADEOFF`: name served attribute + tradeoff; for new service/store probe observability, migration/rollback, error path. 4. Load `ai/directives/agent-inbox/code-interrogation.directive.xml` and run its code-level battery (`NAT`/`IDIOM`/`LIT`/`DEP`/`GLOBAL`/`TEST`/`SEC`/`BIZ`) per changed unit; honour its `ScopeGuard` (no linter-duplication) and `WebSearchGuard` (web-search off by default). Flags attach to the unit's entity card and become ┬¦9 candidates with the probe code in ┬½ą×čüčī┬╗. 5. Assign a verdict per ``; default `unjustified` until disproven (`AX_ADVERSARIAL_DEFAULT`).

1. Skip if no `packages/` (or shared-package root) in the repo. 2. For each candidate run ``; record extract-recommended elements and, conversely, app logic that leaked into a shared package. 3. Report the explicit appŌåöpackage boundary line for the changed area.

Step.Goal: Judge the decomposition as a whole.
Step.Action: 1. Build the element graph: dependency direction, cycles, redundant paths, leaked boundaries, fan-in/out hubs, source-of-truth vs cache edges. 2. Flag relational problems; these are visualized in the output (`AX_VISUALIZE_WHEN_RELATIONAL`), not prose.

Step.Precondition: **MANDATORY DESTINATION CHECK** ŌĆö execute BEFORE any output. Per `AX_VISUAL_TRANSPORT`: 1. Determine destination: is this output going to **chat** (operator reads in Claude/terminal) or **GitLab** (posting to MR)? 2. IF chat ŌåÆ per AX_VISUAL_TRANSPORT Branch CHAT: check for viz tool ŌåÆ use it OR ASCII/emoji-icons. Mermaid FORBIDDEN. 3. IF GitLab ŌåÆ LOAD `ai/directives/agent-inbox/posting-rules.directive.xml`. All diagram conversion and posting format governed by that directive. This directive never posts inline. 4. State the chosen destination and format in the first micro-summary: ┬½ąÆčŗą▓ąŠą┤: čćą░čé, ASCII┬╗ or ┬½ąÆčŗą▓ąŠą┤: čćą░čé, widget┬╗ or ┬½ą¤ąŠčüčéąĖąĮą│: GitLab, Mermaid┬╗.
Step.Action: 1. Choose visual transport per the destination check above (see Precondition). Per `AX_VISUALIZE_WHEN_RELATIONAL`: relational findings ŌåÆ picture; single judgements ŌåÆ prose. Per `AX_SCANNABLE_OUTPUT`: use emoji verdict icons (Ō£ģŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌØī) and risk colours (­¤¤ó­¤¤Ī­¤ö┤). 2. Emit the mandatory scope summary first per `AX_CHANGESET_SCALE` / `` ┬¦2: headline line + by-category table + file map. The file map is rendered for every MR size, never skipped. 3. Render the context/container diagram per `AX_VISUALIZE_WHEN_RELATIONAL` (C4-style: system boundary zone, internal containers coloured by verdict, external systems as black boxes, legend outside the diagram). 4. Add flow diagrams only for non-trivial scenarios; one per scenario, Ōēż7 nodes, one caption each. Omit the section if no non-trivial flow. 5. Emit per-entity cards ordered by reversibility per `AX_REVERSIBILITY_MODULATES_SCRUTINY` (one-way doors first): name ┬Ę role ┬Ę reconstructed rationale ┬Ę verdict with emoji per `AX_SCANNABLE_OUTPUT` ┬Ę stable ID per `AX_STABLE_FINDING_IDS` ┬Ę open question (Q-ID if unresolved). Prose for ŌØī unjustified; one line for Ō£ģŌÜĀ’ĖÅ. 6. Visualize every relational finding from STEP_4 per `AX_VISUALIZE_WHEN_RELATIONAL`; assign R-IDs per `AX_STABLE_FINDING_IDS`. Single-judgement findings stay prose. 7. Reconcile candidates against `prior_threads` / `my_drafts` per `AX_PRIOR_DISCUSSION_AWARENESS`: drop duplicates, convert already-raised into reply-to-thread candidate, mark correction-replies, list awaiting-my-reply threads. 8. Produce verdict summary per `AX_SAY_IT_ONCE`: reference E-IDs and R-IDs, never retell. Table format per `AX_SCANNABLE_OUTPUT`. 9. Produce comment candidate table per `` ┬¦9. Note: posting format governed by `posting-rules.directive.xml` ŌĆö this directive only produces the candidate list, never posts.

ArchInterrogation.OutputFormat:
ONE consolidated report document, assembled in `STEP_5` per `AX_REPORT_ASSEMBLY`. Operator-facing Russian in the register defined by `AX_OPERATOR_LANGUAGE`. Visual transport per `AX_VISUAL_TRANSPORT` mandatory precondition. Scannable structure per `AX_SCANNABLE_OUTPUT`: emoji verdict icons (Ō£ģŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌØī), risk colours (­¤¤ó­¤¤Ī­¤ö┤), `---` between sections, compact tables over prose, details only for critical. Stable IDs per `AX_STABLE_FINDING_IDS` ([E1], [R2], [Q3]). Cross-references per `AX_SAY_IT_ONCE` ŌĆö reference IDs, never retell.
Document structure, top to bottom:
1. **ą©ą░ą┐ą║ą░ [header] ŌĆö mandatory.** First line: `ref` as a clickable link to `webUrl`, then `title`. One click from GitLab before any content. --- 2. **ą×ą▒ąĘąŠčĆ [overview] ŌĆö mandatory scope first.** Open with the quantified scope per `AX_CHANGESET_SCALE`, never just prose: - one headline line ŌĆö `**ąĀą░ąĘą╝ąĄčĆ:** {ą╝ą░ą╗čŗą╣/čüčĆąĄą┤ąĮąĖą╣/ą║čĆčāą┐ąĮčŗą╣} ┬Ę N čäą░ą╣ą╗ąŠą▓ ┬Ę +X / ŌłÆY čüčéčĆąŠą║ ┬Ę M ąĮąŠą▓čŗčģ čäą░ą╣ą╗ąŠą▓ ┬Ę K ąĮąŠą▓čŗčģ čüčāčēąĮąŠčüčéąĄą╣` ŌĆö plus a half-sentence read: expansion (new files/entities) or churn, and the dominant category; - a by-category table (rows: ąÜąŠą┤ ┬Ę ąóąĄčüčéčŗ ┬Ę ąöąŠą║ąĖ/čüą┐ąĄą║ąĖ ┬Ę ąÜąŠąĮčäąĖą│ ┬Ę ąÉčüčüąĄčéčŗ; columns: ążą░ą╣ą╗ąŠą▓ ┬Ę +čüčéčĆąŠą║ ┬Ę ŌłÆčüčéčĆąŠą║ ┬Ę ąĮąŠą▓čŗčģ), so a big `+X` driven by assets/generated files is not misread as logic; - a **file map** (mandatory, every size): touched files grouped by folder/module, each with change-type icon (­¤åĢ added ┬Ę Ō£Å’ĖÅ modified ┬Ę ­¤Śæ’ĖÅ deleted ┬Ę Ōå¬’ĖÄ renamed) and ┬▒ lines ŌĆö a few-line list for a tiny MR, a grouped tree for a large one; Then 2ŌĆō4 sentences: what the MR introduces and the verdict tally per `AX_STABLE_FINDING_IDS` (N Ō£ģ justified, N ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ weak, N ŌØī unjustified). No findings yet ŌĆö just the shape and the scale. --- 3. **ąÉčĆčģąĖčéąĄą║čéčāčĆą░ [helicopter].** The C4-style container diagram per `AX_VISUALIZE_WHEN_RELATIONAL` (boundary zone, internal containers coloured by verdict Ō£ģŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌØī, external systems as black boxes, legend outside). One caption sentence under it. --- 4. **ą¤ąŠčéąŠą║ąĖ [flows].** Flow diagram(s) for non-trivial scenarios only; one per scenario, Ōēż7 nodes, one caption each. Omit the section if no non-trivial flow. --- 5. **ąĀą░ąĘą▒ąŠčĆ čüčāčēąĮąŠčüčéąĄą╣ [entities].** One subsection per entity, ordered by reversibility per `AX_REVERSIBILITY_MODULATES_SCRUTINY` (one-way doors first). Each card: - **Stable ID** ŌĆö `[E1]`, `[E2]`ŌĆ” per `AX_STABLE_FINDING_IDS`. - **Role** ŌĆö one sentence. - **Verdict** ŌĆö emoji icon per `AX_SCANNABLE_OUTPUT`: Ō£ģ justified / ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ weak / ŌØī unjustified + one-line reason. - **Rationale** ŌĆö reconstructed per `AX_RECONSTRUCT_RATIONALE`, incl. ┬½ą┐ąŠčćąĄą╝čā ąĮąĄ ą░ą╗čīčéąĄčĆąĮą░čéąĖą▓ą░┬╗. - **Open question** ŌĆö `[Q1]` if unresolved, per `AX_STABLE_FINDING_IDS`. Prose for ŌØī unjustified; one compact line for Ō£ģŌÜĀ’ĖÅ per `AX_SCANNABLE_OUTPUT` rule 5. --- 6. **ąĪą▓čÅąĘąĖ ąĖ čĆąĖčüą║ąĖ [relationships].** Cross-entity findings with R-IDs per `AX_STABLE_FINDING_IDS`. Visualized where relational per `AX_VISUALIZE_WHEN_RELATIONAL`; one caption each. Single-judgement findings stay prose.
Risk assessment ŌĆö fixed sub-items adapted for architecture review, in this order: - ­¤Ä» **ąÜąŠąĮčåąĄąĮčéčĆą░čåąĖčÅ ąĖąĘą╝ąĄąĮąĄąĮąĖą╣** ŌĆö ą│ą┤ąĄ čüąŠčüčĆąĄą┤ąŠčéąŠčćąĄąĮčŗ ąĖąĘą╝ąĄąĮąĄąĮąĖčÅ (čäą░ą╣ą╗čŗ/ą╝ąŠą┤čāą╗ąĖ čü ąĮą░ąĖą▒ąŠą╗čīčłąĖą╝ churn). - ­¤¦¬ **ą¤ąŠą║čĆčŗčéąĖąĄ čéąĄčüčéą░ą╝ąĖ** ŌĆö ąĄčüčéčī ą╗ąĖ čéąĄčüčéčŗ čā ąĘą░čéčĆąŠąĮčāčéčŗčģ čäą░ą╣ą╗ąŠą▓; ą┐ąŠą▓ąĄą┤ąĄąĮąĖąĄ ąĖąĘą╝ąĄąĮąĄąĮąŠ ą▒ąĄąĘ čéąĄčüčéąŠą▓ = čĆąĖčüą║. - ­¤Æź **Blast-radius** ŌĆö ą║čéąŠ ąĘą░ą▓ąĖčüąĖčé ąŠčé ąĖąĘą╝ąĄąĮčæąĮąĮčŗčģ čüčāčēąĮąŠčüčéąĄą╣ (čüą╝. [R-IDs]). - ­¤¢É **ą¤čĆąŠą▓ąĄčĆąĖčéčī čĆčāą║ą░ą╝ąĖ** ŌĆö ąĘą░čéčĆąŠąĮčāčéčŗąĄ čäą╗ąŠčā, ą║čĆąĖčéąĖčćąĄčüą║ąĖąĄ ą│čĆą░ąĮąĖčåčŗ/čāčüą╗ąŠą▓ąĖčÅ. - ­¤ÜĆ **ąĀąĖčüą║ąĖ čĆąĄą╗ąĖąĘą░** ŌĆö ą╝ąĖą│čĆą░čåąĖąĖ, ąŠą▒čĆą░čéąĮą░čÅ čüąŠą▓ą╝ąĄčüčéąĖą╝ąŠčüčéčī, čüą╝ąĄąĮą░ API, ą┤ąĄą┐-ą▒ą░ą╝ą┐, env/ą║ąŠąĮčäąĖą│. - ­¤ōŖ **ąŻčĆąŠą▓ąĄąĮčī čĆąĖčüą║ą░** ŌĆö ­¤¤ó low / ­¤¤Ī medium / ­¤ö┤ high (mandatory conclusion, čüčüčŗą╗ą░ąĄčéčüčÅ ąĮą░ [E-IDs] ąĖ [R-IDs] ą▓čŗčłąĄ). --- 7. **ąśčéąŠą│ ąĖ čĆąĄą║ąŠą╝ąĄąĮą┤ą░čåąĖąĖ [verdict + extraction].** Per `AX_SAY_IT_ONCE`: reference E-IDs and R-IDs, never retell. Table: verdict summary (Ō£ģ N / ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ N / ŌØī N), package-extraction recommendations per `AX_PACKAGE_EXTRACTION_PROBE`, explicit appŌåöframework boundary line. --- 8. **ąĪčāčēąĄčüčéą▓čāčÄčēąĄąĄ ąŠą▒čüčāąČą┤ąĄąĮąĖąĄ [prior discussion] ŌĆö only when `prior_threads`/`my_drafts` supplied.** Per `AX_PRIOR_DISCUSSION_AWARENESS`: what other reviewers have already raised (so it is clear what is NOT re-raised below), which threads mention me or await my reply, and which of my own notes/drafts already cover a point. Omit the section entirely when no prior discussion was supplied, and say once in ┬½ą×ą▒ąĘąŠčĆ┬╗ that the analysis ran without prior-discussion context. --- 9. **ąÜą░ąĮą┤ąĖą┤ą░čéčŗ ą▓ ąĘą░ą╝ąĄčćą░ąĮąĖčÅ [comment candidates].** Per `AX_PRIOR_DISCUSSION_AWARENESS`. Compact table per `AX_SCANNABLE_OUTPUT`:
| ID | ążą░ą╣ą╗ | ąĪčéčĆąŠą║ą░ | ą¤čĆąŠą▒ą╗ąĄą╝ą░ | ą×čüčī | Kind | |----|------|--------|-----------|-----|------| | [E3] | path/to/file.ts | L42 | ą║čĆą░čéą║ą░čÅ čüčāčéčī | C | new-line-comment | | [E5] | path/to/file.ts | L15 | ą║čĆą░čéą║ą░čÅ čüčāčéčī | GLOBAL | reply-to-thread (discussionId: abc123) |
┬½ą×čüčī┬╗ ŌĆö ą╗ąĖą▒ąŠ ą░čĆčģąĖčéąĄą║čéčāčĆąĮą░čÅ ąŠčüčī `A`ŌĆō`G`, ą╗ąĖą▒ąŠ ą║ąŠą┤-ą┐čĆąŠą▒ą░ ąĖąĘ `code-interrogation.directive.xml` (`NAT`/`IDIOM`/`LIT`/`DEP`/`GLOBAL`/`TEST`/`SEC`/`BIZ`). Kind values per `AX_PRIOR_DISCUSSION_AWARENESS`: `new-line-comment` / `reply-to-thread` (discussionId) / `correction-reply` / `awaiting-my-reply`.
**IMPORTANT:** This directive produces the candidate list ONLY. Posting format (Mermaid, ­¤ż¢ prefix, vcs-reply syntax, line-comment position fields, granularity by artifact type) is governed by `ai/directives/agent-inbox/posting-rules.directive.xml`. When the skill proceeds to posting, it MUST load that directive. --- 10. **ą¤ąŠą┤ą▓ą░ą╗ [footer] ŌĆö mandatory.** Repeats the `ref`ŌåÆ`webUrl` link so the operator jumps to GitLab without scrolling up.
